Minimum Average(s) Required.General degree: A minimum cumulative overall average of 53.0%.
Minimum Average(s) Required.Honours degrees: A minimum cumulative overall average of 63.0%.
Minimum Average(s) Required.notes: See major for required major average.

Time to Completion.standard: Students must successfully complete all degree requirements within eight calendar years from the time students first enter their plan.
exceptions: Students admitted with the equivalent of one year advanced standing must complete the degree requirements in seven calendar years of entry into the plan.
Students admitted with the equivalent of two years of advanced standing must complete the degree requirements in six calendar years of entry into the plan.

Undergraduate Communication Requirement.description: Students are required to complete one course to meet the Undergraduate Communication Requirement.
notes: Students enrolled in the Faculty of Health before September 2017 should consult the English Language Proficiency Requirement described in the Calendar of their entry year.
Successful completion of the Undergraduate Communication Requirement will be recorded on a student's transcript.
Students transferring to another faculty may need additional courses to satisfy that faculty's Undergraduate Communication Requirement.

Students transferring into the Faculty of Health who have completed their Undergraduate Communication Requirement in another faculty at the University of Waterloo may have it count toward this requirement.
External transfer credits for HEALTH107 are not permitted; it must be taken at the University of Waterloo.

requirements: PD1: Must be taken in an academic term prior to the first work term.
PD12: Must be taken during the first work term.
Two additional PD courses: Taken during the second, third, or fourth work terms.

Additional Constraints and Notes: Students must be in an honours academic plan to be eligible for co-op.
The co-op program is only open to full-time students.
The co-op program is not open to students completing a second degree.
Students will be removed from co-op for failing academic requirements or having two unemployed/failed work terms.
Normally, students removed from co-op will not be re-admitted.
